description: Maps scenario IDs to branch names and machine-readable expected outcomes. Revert scenarios (4.x) map to gh-audit's Architecture.md rule 8. Each scenario's `assertions` array is the source of truth for automated validation (see scripts/test-fixtures.sh in gh-audit). Other fields are descriptive.

4.1.title: AutoRevert (bot-style message)
4.1.notes: Message matches `Automatic revert of <sha>..<sha>` regex; trusted by construction without diff verification.

4.5.title: GH 'Revert' button — clean, squash-merged
4.5.notes: GH squash commit has no `This reverts commit <sha>` trailer; reverted SHA is recovered from the revert PR's head branch `revert-29-…` → PR #29's merge_commit_sha → diff-verify.

4.6.title: GH 'Revert' button with conflict resolution
4.6.notes: Not reproducible — GitHub refuses the Revert button when the base can't be auto-reverted. Users in this situation fall back to local `git revert`, covered by 4.3.

4.7.title: GH 'Revert' button, merge-commit strategy
4.7.notes: The revert commit lives on the second-parent chain of the 2-parent `Merge pull request #…` commit. gh-audit audits both. The merge commit is NotRevert (CleanMerge-shaped). The revert commit itself gets the branch-name fallback treatment and diff-verifies.

assertions.comment: underlying revert commit in second-parent chain

4.8.title: Revert-of-revert via the GH UI (UI-only)
4.8.notes: Produced by clicking Revert on PR #33 (the 4.5 revert) and merge-committing. The revert-of-revert content lives in the second-parent chain of the merge commit.

assertions.comment: revert-of-revert via GH UI — never classified clean

4.9.title: Revert-of-revert via local git (legacy message format)
4.9.notes: First revert (5bc11db) is clean — asserted compliant. Revert-of-revert (4490b9d) re-applies bytes and must be reviewed — asserted non-compliant.

4.10.title: Direct-push clean revert (no PR)
4.10.notes: Rule 3 (no associated PR) wins over rule 8 (clean-revert waiver). The revert's diff verifies cleanly and the flag persists on the audit row, but the verdict is non-compliant because direct-push-by-human bypasses review entirely.

4.11.title: Octopus merge (3-way merge)
4.11.notes: Not a revert scenario per se — exercises the octopus path. The octopus commit has no PR and has OctopusMerge kind, which is not auto-classified as clean.

4.12.title: Empty revert of empty base
4.12.notes: Empty-commit fallback fires before rule 8 so the compliant reason is `empty commit` rather than `clean revert`; verdict is the same.

1.1.title: Squash merge — no review
1.1.notes: Squash-merged PR with no reviews. Tests commit→PR linking for squash strategy and confirms non-compliance when no approval exists.

1.2.title: Merge commit (create merge commit) — no review
1.2.notes: Standard 2-parent merge commit via the GitHub merge button. Verified signature from web-flow means CleanMerge classification. Non-compliant due to no approval.

1.3.title: Rebase merge — no review
1.3.notes: Rebase-merged PR. GitHub rewrites the commit SHA on rebase, so the audited commit is the rebased SHA (not the original branch commit). Tests commit→PR linking for rebase strategy.

2.1.title: No approval — PR merged without reviews
2.1.notes: PR merged with zero reviews of any kind. Simplest non-compliant case: rule 4 fires because no approval exists on the final commit.

3.1.title: COMMENT-only review (not an approval)
3.1.notes: PR with only a COMMENT review (no APPROVE or REQUEST_CHANGES). COMMENT reviews are informational and don't constitute approval on GitHub, so the commit is non-compliant. Tests that gh-audit correctly ignores COMMENTED state for approval purposes.

2.2.title: Self-approval (reviewer is code contributor)
2.2.notes: sjainepenner pushes code to the PR branch then approves. Since they are both a reviewer and a PR-branch commit author, it's self-approval. Non-compliant.

2.3.title: Stale approval (approved on old SHA)
2.3.notes: Reviewer approves on the first commit, then author pushes more code. Approval is on an older SHA, not the final commit. Non-compliant with has_stale_approval=true.

2.4.title: Independent approval (compliant happy path)
2.4.notes: sjainepenner approves stefanpenner's PR on the final commit. The gold-standard compliant case: independent reviewer, approval on head SHA.

2.5.title: Post-merge concern (CHANGES_REQUESTED after merge)
2.5.notes: Reviewer approves and merge goes through. Reviewer then submits CHANGES_REQUESTED after merge. Commit is still compliant (point-in-time compliance) but has_post_merge_concern=true.

3.2.title: APPROVE then DISMISS
3.2.notes: Reviewer approves, then the approval is dismissed by the repo owner. The latest state is DISMISSED, so no valid approval exists. Non-compliant.

3.3.title: APPROVED then COMMENTED (approval survives)
3.3.notes: Reviewer approves, then posts a COMMENT review. Per GitHub's model (and gh-audit's latestReviewStatesOnFinal), COMMENTED does not clobber APPROVED. Compliant.
